meetingType: Joint Public Hearing — Economic Development and University Relations Committee and Finance Committee

items.title: Harvard 'Harvard in the Community' presentation to joint EDUR + Finance hearing on PILOT agreement
items.summary: The Economic Development and University Relations Committee and the Finance Committee held a joint public hearing with Harvard University to discuss the Harvard Payment in Lieu of Taxes (PILOT) agreement. As part of that hearing, the committees received a 'Harvard in the Community' presentation from Thomas J. Lucey of Harvard Public Affairs & Communications outlining Harvard's CPS partnerships, housing collaborations, employment of Cambridge residents, public-realm investments in Harvard Square, and direct payments to the City. No Council vote was taken; the presentation was received as a communication.
items.body:
A presentation has been received from Thomas J. Lucey Senior Director of Government & Community Relations Harvard Public Affairs & Communications entitled "Harvard in Community".
COM 2026-20

Updated Call of meeting: The Economic Development and University Relations Committee and Finance Committee will hold a joint public hearing with Harvard University to discuss and receive an update on the Harvard University Payment in Lieu of Taxes (PILOT) agreement.

history.action: FIRST IN COUNCIL — presentation received at joint EDUR + Finance Committee public hearing on Harvard PILOT agreement

rationale.doc.kind:COM: Section II. COMMUNICATIONS; bold ID line 'COM 2026-20' on attestation page
rationale.doc.item_id:com-2026-20: Bold ID line 'COM 2026-20 FIRST IN COUNCIL April 30, 2026'
rationale.doc.meeting_date:2026-04-30: Cover page header 'AGENDA APRIL 30, 2026' confirms meeting date
rationale.geo.scope:citywide: Harvard's community engagement, employment, and PILOT impact span all of Cambridge; no single neighborhood is the subject
rationale.topic.area:schools: Multiple slides on 'Partnering with Cambridge Public Schools', Project TEACH, CRLS, Cambridge Harvard Summer Academy
rationale.household.kids:k12: 'Harvard's educational programs are available in every CPS school for all CPS grade levels' and CRLS / 7th-grader programs
rationale.household.school:cps: 'Partnering with Cambridge Public Schools' — programs in every CPS school
rationale.work.employer:university: 'Harvard has been the largest employer in Cambridge for 25 consecutive years'
rationale.work.audience:cambridge_workers: '4,413 Cambridge residents directly employed by Harvard; $517M in wages, salaries, and benefits paid to Cambridge residents in FY25'
rationale.fiscal.kind:property_tax: Joint hearing subject is the Harvard 'Payment in Lieu of Taxes (PILOT) agreement' — payments substituting for property tax on Harvard's tax-exempt land
rationale.civic.flag:public_comment_open: 'will hold a joint public hearing with Harvard University' — public hearing scheduled at this meeting
